attempts.test_code:
import math
from source import sqrt

def test_sqrt_convergence_uses_squared_residual():
    """
    The convergence check must verify |approx^2 - x| < epsilon, not |x - approx| < epsilon.
    For sqrt(2, 0.01), the buggy criterion |x - approx| > epsilon never terminates because
    |2 - sqrt(2)| ≈ 0.586, which is always > 0.01. The fixed code converges correctly.
    """
    result = sqrt(2, 0.01)
    expected = math.sqrt(2)
    
    # Result must be within epsilon of the true square root (postcondition)
    assert abs(result - expected) < 0.01, \
        f"sqrt(2, 0.01) returned {result:.8f}, expected within 0.01 of {expected:.8f}"
